python_examples.name: Using requests library
python_examples.code:
import requests

url = "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/recommendations"
payload = {
    "skill": "python programming",
    "difficulty": "beginner",
    "max_results": 10,
    "preferences": ["web development", "django"]
}

response = requests.post(url, json=payload)
data = response.json()

print(f"Found {data['total_results']} recommendations")
for course in data['recommendations']:
    print(f"- {course['title']} ({course['provider']})")

python_examples.name: Using httpx (async)
python_examples.code:
import httpx
import asyncio

async def get_recommendations():
    url = "http://127.0.0.1:8000/api/recommendations"
    payload = {
        "skill": "javascript",
        "difficulty": "intermediate",
        "max_results": 5
    }
    
    async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:
        response = await client.post(url, json=payload)
        return response.json()

data = asyncio.run(get_recommendations())
print(data)

preferences.description: Additional keywords or preferences to refine recommendations
